GenAiFunction.description:
Persist a learned user preference so it is remembered in future conversations (loaded by LoadCustomInstructions at session start).
Use when the user signals a durable preference: - Corrects you: "No, use Revenue not Amount", "That's not what I meant", "Actually..." - Refines output in a way worth keeping: "Sort that by close date", "Can you also include..." - States a preference: "always", "from now on", "I prefer", "don't do X" - Shows dissatisfaction and rephrases: "not quite", "try again", "wrong".
NOT for: - One-off requests that aren't meant to stick ("for this one report, sort by date") — just honor them without storing. - Loading previous preferences — that's LoadCustomInstructions.
Format memories as OBSERVATIONS about the user, not commands to yourself: - Good: "When showing opportunities, user prefers Amount and CloseDate included" - Bad: "When showing opportunities, include Amount and CloseDate"
Include a short example reference when it makes the memory more actionable: - With example: "When showing pipeline, user prefers tables (like the January forecast)" - Without: "When showing pipeline, user prefers tables with Amount, Stage, CloseDate"
Inputs: - summary: max 80 chars, e.g. "Prefers tables for pipeline data". - instruction: max 255 chars, pattern "When [context], user prefers [preference]" + optional example.
After storing, confirm briefly and apply the preference immediately in this conversation.
